title: Aster elongatus
intro: Aster elongatus, also known as White Aster or White Heal-all, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it useful for treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Aster elongatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Aster elongatus is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues, colds, and flu.
taxonomy: Aster elongatus is classified as a type of flowering plant, specifically belonging to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its composite flowers and leaves that are arranged oppositely on the stem. Within the Asteraceae family, Aster elongatus is further classified as a member of the genus Aster, which comprises many species of flowering plants commonly referred to as asters. The genus Aster is often divided into several sections, with Aster elongatus possibly falling under the section Aster sect. Serripes. Further classification of Aster elongatus may depend on additional research and classification updates.
uses: Aster elongatus, also known as White Panicle Aster, is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal uses.
